Test Description: Configures DMA descriptors for 8 channels, enables per-channel interrupts, starts transfers via debug instructions, waits for interrupt, and invokes data_integrity over each channel's source/destination buffers.
Remarks: Build-time selection via MPS_DMA or APS_DMA controls the IRQ number and the SYSREG interrupt enable register used.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point: test_case()
1) Compute iter = DATA_LEN_IN_BYTES / (SRC_BURST * (SRC_SIZE_IN_BITS/8)).
2) Initialize arrays: src_addr[8], dst_addr[8], desc_addr[8], desc_addr_actual[8] with constants defined at top of file.
3) If MPS_DMA is defined: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(0x7).
4) If MPS_DMA is defined: WRITE MIZAR_MPS_SYSREG_INTR_EN = 0x80 via write_reg().
5) Else if APS_DMA is defined: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(0x17).
6) Else if APS_DMA is defined: WRITE MIZAR_APS_SYSREG_INTR_EN = 0x80 via write_reg().
7) For j from 0 to 7: CALL initialize_memory(desc_addr[i]).
8) printf(Inside Testcase\n").

SS / Module: MIZAR_MPS_SYSREG_INTR_EN, MIZAR_APS_SYSREG_INTR_EN, MIZAR_DMA_INTEN, MIZAR_DMA_DBGINST0, MIZAR_DMA_DBGINST1, MIZAR_DMA_DBGCMD

Test Description: Default value verification for all listed DMA registers and masked write-read verification across 6 data patterns; aggregates failures and returns overall pass/fail.
Remarks: Addresses are conditionally skipped when: skip_array[i]==1, or read_mask_array[i]==0x00000000 (for reads), or write_mask_array[i]==0x00000000 (for writes).
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point: test_case()
1) CALL chk_rst_val().
   1.1) For i from 0 to (CNT-1):
        a) addr = addr_array[i].
        b) IF (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) THEN: print skip message; CONTINUE.
        c) READ data_rd = read_reg(addr).
        d) IF (data_rd == default_value_array[i]) THEN: (optional debug) print PASS.
           ELSE: def_fail_cnt++ and print failure with expected vs read values.
2) (Optional debug) printf end of default value check.
3) CALL chk_rd_wr().
   3.1) Define chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff, 0xaaaaaaaa, 0x55555555, 0x00000000, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xffff0000}.
   3.2) For j from 0 to 5 (each pattern): set data_wr = chk_val[j].
        Writing phase (for i from 0 to CNT-1):
        a) addr = addr_array[i].
        b) IF (skip_array[i] == 1) THEN: print skip message; CONTINUE.
        c) IF (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) THEN: print not-writable; CONTINUE.
        d) ELSE: WRITE register at addr with data_wr via write_reg(addr, data_wr); (optional debug) print info.
        Reading/verification phase (for i from 0 to CNT-1):
        e) addr = addr_array[i].
        f) IF (skip_array[i] == 1) THEN: print skip message; CONTINUE.
        g) IF (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) THEN: print not-writable; CONTINUE.
        h) IF (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) THEN: print not-readable; CONTINUE.
        i) ELSE: READ data_rd = read_reg(addr).
           Compute wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff).
           Compute exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])).
           IF (data_rd == exp_val) THEN: (optional debug) print PASS.
           ELSE: wr_fail_cnt++ and print failure with expected vs read values.
4) (Optional debug) printf end of write & read section.
5) IF (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) THEN: CALL finish(1) ELSE: CALL finish(0).
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_DMA_DSR, MIZAR_DMA_DPC, MIZAR_DMA_INTEN, MIZAR_DMA_INT_EVENT_RIS, MIZAR_DMA_INTMIS, MIZAR_DMA_INTCLR, MIZAR_DMA_FSRD, MIZAR_DMA_FSRC, MIZAR_DMA_FTRD, MIZAR_DMA_FTR0, MIZAR_DMA_FTR1, MIZAR_DMA_FTR2, MIZAR_DMA_FTR3, MIZAR_DMA_FTR4, MIZAR_DMA_FTR5, MIZAR_DMA_FTR6, MIZAR_DMA_FTR7, MIZAR_DMA_CSR0, MIZAR_DMA_CSR1, MIZAR_DMA_CSR2, MIZAR_DMA_CSR3, MIZAR_DMA_CSR4, MIZAR_DMA_CSR5, MIZAR_DMA_CSR6, MIZAR_DMA_CSR7, MIZAR_DMA_CPC0, MIZAR_DMA_CPC1, MIZAR_DMA_CPC2, MIZAR_DMA_CPC3, MIZAR_DMA_CPC4, MIZAR_DMA_CPC5, MIZAR_DMA_CPC6, MIZAR_DMA_CPC7, MIZAR_DMA_SAR0, MIZAR_DMA_SAR1, MIZAR_DMA_SAR2, MIZAR_DMA_SAR3, MIZAR_DMA_SAR4, MIZAR_DMA_SAR5, MIZAR_DMA_SAR6, MIZAR_DMA_SAR7, MIZAR_DMA_DAR0, MIZAR_DMA_DAR1, MIZAR_DMA_DAR2, MIZAR_DMA_DAR3, MIZAR_DMA_DAR4, MIZAR_DMA_DAR5, MIZAR_DMA_DAR6, MIZAR_DMA_DAR7, MIZAR_DMA_CCR0, MIZAR_DMA_CCR1, MIZAR_DMA_CCR2, MIZAR_DMA_CCR3, MIZAR_DMA_CCR4, MIZAR_DMA_CCR5, MIZAR_DMA_CCR6, MIZAR_DMA_CCR7, MIZAR_DMA_LC0_0, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_1, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_2, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_3, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_4, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_5, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_6, MIZAR_DMA_LCO_7, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_0, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_1, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_2, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_3, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_4, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_5, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_6, MIZAR_DMA_LC1_7, MIZAR_DMA_DBGSTATUS, MIZAR_DMA_DBGCMD, MIZAR_DMA_DBGINST0, MIZAR_DMA_DBGINST1, MIZAR_DMA_CR0, MIZAR_DMA_CR1, MIZAR_DMA_CR2, MIZAR_DMA_CR3, MIZAR_DMA_CR4, MIZAR_DMA_CRD, MIZAR_DMA_WD, MIZAR_DMA_PERIPH_ID_0, MIZAR_DMA_PERIPH_ID_1, MIZAR_DMA_PERIPH_ID_2, MIZAR_DMA_PERIPH_ID_3, MIZAR_DMA_PCELL_ID_0, MIZAR_DMA_PCELL_ID_1, MIZAR_DMA_PCELL_ID_2, MIZAR_DMA_PCELL_ID_3
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Default value check: For each i where read_mask_array[i] != 0, check (read_reg(addr_array[i]) == default_value_array[i]); PASS when equal; FAIL otherwise (increments def_fail_cnt and prints failure message).
- Write/Read check per pattern and address i where skip_array[i]==0 and both write_mask_array[i] and read_mask_array[i] are non-zero: After WRITE write_reg(addr_array[i], data_wr), verify data_rd = read_reg(addr_array[i]) equals exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | ((write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff) & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); PASS when equal; FAIL otherwise (increments wr_fail_cnt and prints failure message).
- Final acceptance: PASS if (def_fail_cnt == 0 && wr_fail_cnt == 0); otherwise FAIL (finish(1)).
